title: Analytic pit-economics features plus regime-specific sigmoid stacking
summary: Strategy references emphasize undercut thresholds, traffic penalties, and approximate optimal stint length from pit-loss vs degradation slope.
rationale: Strategy references emphasize undercut thresholds, traffic penalties, and approximate optimal stint length from pit-loss vs degradation slope. Those formulas give testable, interpretable features; separately, sigmoid calibration is monotone and preserves ranking better than isotonic, making it safer for ROC-AUC-focused blends.
implementation_hint: Derive per-row features such as estimated degradation slope, `N*≈sqrt(2P/d)`, distance to optimal stop lap, and undercut margin using field-relative pace proxies and track-position context. Train separate dry and wet/intermediate specialists if needed, then blend their OOF scores with the baseline using logistic stacking or Platt scaling, not isotonic.
expected_effect: Adds a more structural notion of ‘economic stop urgency’ and may improve leaderboard stability through safer monotone blending.
risk: Pit-loss `P` is unobserved and must be approximated from race/lap context; bad approximations could make these features brittle. Overfitting the blender on non-held-out folds is also a real leakage path.
